title: Source lineage for The Chronicle of England, Part 1
summary: The checked-in artifact is the pinned CME copy of U-M item AHB1379.0001.001, an uncorrected OCR encoding with 880 linked page-image references made from Frederick James Furnivall's two separate 1887 volume expressions. Furnivall's first-part text ends at printed line 16,688. He takes lines 1-198 and a finite set of later main-text supplies from Inner Temple Petyt 511.7, uses Lambeth Palace Library MS 131 as the principal base from line 199 with those supplies excluded, and collates Petyt throughout. Bodleian Rawlinson D.913 fol. 4 independently preserves target lines 13,018-13,193. Merton 23.b.6 is explicitly Part II; Gloucester Cathedral MS 36 fragment 8 is not assigned to Part I without a direct scope. The two Internet Archive scans, U-M page-image layer, five-image Lambeth selection, and ten-canvas Rawlinson object are distinct access and facsimile layers.
review_notes: The target is one bounded Part I work through printed line 16,688, not the entire two-part DIMEV record, the whole Furnivall publication, or all BODY content in the U-M encoding.
Furnivall's two physical 1887 volumes are modeled as separate edition expressions, and each Internet Archive scan is a separate facsimile carrier.

The Lambeth five-image IIIF object is complete only as a provider selection; it is partial coverage of MS 131 and of the target. The Rawlinson ten-canvas IIIF object is complete as a provider selection but omits target fol. 4 entirely.

Access, copyright status, licenses, and provider reproduction conditions are separate assertions. The U-M NoC-US designation is not conflated with the legacy XML AVAILABILITY warning or with the unresolved scope of the CME repository's Unlicense file.
No date more precise than the directly supported source permits is assigned to Rawlinson D.913 fol. 4, and no exact Part I overlap is assigned to Gloucester fragment 8.

The current Medictionary XML is a distinct structural version of the U-M-derived encoding: its BODY text is identical after whitespace normalization and its core BODY/LG/L/PB/MILESTONE counts agree, but it changes the document wrapper, note and emphasis element names, front-matter modeling, attributes, and availability statement.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for ahb1379
entities.description: The exact pinned XML artifact consumed by this repository through the Kyvero-Press/CME submodule.
notes: The reviewed file is 1,379,542 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AHB1379.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 Level-1 TEI-in-Libraries encoding produced by uncorrected OCR from Furnivall's two-volume edition. The header says no OCR corrections or content editing were performed and links 880 600-dpi TIFF page references.
notes: The header's eng code describes the collection encoding; the medieval target is normalized to enm in descriptive metadata.

entities.label: Medictionary current XML for ahb1379
entities.description: A currently retrievable structural version of the U-M-derived text. Its BODY text is byte-different because of layout but identical after whitespace normalization; it retains 278 BODY DIV1, 436 LG, 16,974 L, 880 PB, and 637 MILESTONE. It changes DLPSTEXTCLASS to ETS/EEBO, flattens two TITLEPAGE structures into FRONT DIV1, renames NOTE1/NOTE2 to NOTE with PLACE values, Q1 to Q, and HI1 to HI, removes EPB/LB and several source attributes, and replaces the legacy availability warning with a public-domain statement.
notes: The added IDG value CME00000 is a generic transformed identifier and is not used as the work_id.
The changed availability paragraph is evidence about the underlying U-M-derived work, not a separate license for Medictionary-specific markup.

entities.label: Furnivall, The Story of England (1887), two-volume edition
entities.description: The aggregate 1887 scholarly edition containing two distinct physical volume expressions. The bounded Part I target spans volume I pp. 1-454 and volume II pp. 455-579, ending at line 16,688.

bibliographic.citation: The Story of England by Robert Manning of Brunne, A.D. 1338, ed. Frederick James Furnivall, 2 vols., Rerum Britannicarum Medii Aevi Scriptores 87 (London: Longman & Co., 1887).
bibliographic.title: The Story of England by Robert Manning of Brunne, A.D. 1338
bibliographic.pages: Part I target pp. 1-579 across volumes I-II

entities.label: Furnivall, The Story of England, volume I (1887)
entities.description: The first physical volume expression of Furnivall's Rolls Series edition. It contains target printed pp. 1-454, corresponding to lines 1-13,108.

bibliographic.citation: The Story of England by Robert Manning of Brunne, A.D. 1338, ed. Frederick James Furnivall, vol. I, Rerum Britannicarum Medii Aevi Scriptores 87 (London: Longman & Co., 1887).
bibliographic.title: The Story of England by Robert Manning of Brunne, A.D. 1338

entities.label: Furnivall, The Story of England, volume II (1887)
entities.description: The second physical volume expression of Furnivall's edition. It resumes at printed p. 455 / target line 13,109, carries Part I through line 16,688 on p. 579, and then prints the Part II transition through line 16,730 on p. 580.

bibliographic.citation: The Story of England by Robert Manning of Brunne, A.D. 1338, ed. Frederick James Furnivall, vol. II, Rerum Britannicarum Medii Aevi Scriptores 87 (London: Longman & Co., 1887).
bibliographic.title: The Story of England by Robert Manning of Brunne, A.D. 1338
bibliographic.pages: Target pp. 455-579; excluded Part II transition pp. 579-580

entities.label: Inner Temple Library, Petyt 511.7, Part VII
entities.description: A complete manuscript witness of the two-part chronicle. Furnivall uses it for target lines 1-198, exact later supplies, and continuing collation; the target occupies principally fols. 1ra-95va and Part II begins on fol. 95b.

holding.notes: DIMEV records the complete chronicle at fols. 1ra-195.

notes: Furnivall reports one hand for the target through fol. 95va.
No precise production date is normalized in this draft because the directly reviewed access page does not supply one.

Collateral reproduction routes reported by the reviewed sources are the 1978 World Microfilms copy and a MED photostat at Bentley Historical Library, MED Records Box 70. Neither is Furnivall's source intermediary, and current availability or reuse permission was not verified.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library, MS 131
entities.description: The principal manuscript base for Furnivall's Part I text from line 199, except for enumerated Petyt supplies and lacuna repairs. The first leaf is lost, so the surviving manuscript begins at Furnivall line 199.

notes: James describes two hands and loss of the first leaf. Furnivall's historical dating is not used over the later catalog.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Rawlinson D.913
entities.description: The whole physical composite guardbook, assembled around 1861, that contains the target witness fragment on fol. 4r-v corresponding to Furnivall lines 13,018-13,193. Other leaves in the carrier preserve unrelated material; the target claim is always scoped to fol. 4 rather than extended to the composite as a whole.

holding.notes: Target fragment at fol. 4; official MARCO record describes the composite volume.

physical_description.extent: composite guardbook; target fragment at fol. 4r-v
physical_description.format: multiple manuscript fragments bound as one physical carrier
notes: The public ten-canvas Digital Bodleian selection covers non-target fols. 23r-43r in this same carrier.
The target fragment's production date remains unresolved; no aggregate MARCO date is silently transferred to fol. 4.

entities.label: Merton College, MS 23.b.6, pastedown fragment
entities.description: A reported witness explicitly scoped by DIMEV to Part II, lines 2,040-2,239. It is not a source witness for the bounded Part I target.
notes: Part II scope is explicit; this entity is retained to prevent aggregate witness lists from being misread as Part I ancestry.

entities.label: Gloucester Cathedral Library, MS 36, fragment 8
entities.description: A DIMEV-reported fragment of the aggregate two-part chronicle whose exact textual scope was not established from a direct catalog or image.
notes: It is not bound to Part I and is not called a target witness until direct scope evidence is obtained.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan storyengland00offigoog
entities.description: A complete scan carrier for Furnivall volume I. The item metadata identifies a Google Books source and marks possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, scan-data XML, and metadata JSON

entities.label: Internet Archive scan storyofenglandby0000unse_j9k4
entities.description: A complete scan carrier for Furnivall volume II, including target pp. 455-579 and the following Part II transition.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, scan-data XML, and metadata JSON

entities.label: U-M page-image layer for AHB1379.0001.001
entities.description: The separate U-M image layer referenced by 880 REF/SEQ links in the XML header and PB milestones. It is distinct from the OCR text and from both Internet Archive carriers.

entities.label: Lambeth MS 131 five-image LUNA selection
entities.description: The complete five-image provider selection for fols. 1r, 23v, 26v, 39v, and 42v. It is partial coverage of the manuscript and of Part I, and is not a whole-manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: five LUNA Image records and five 1800-pixel IIIF JPEG derivatives

entities.label: Digital Bodleian Rawlinson D.913 ten-canvas selection
entities.description: A complete ten-canvas provider-selected IIIF object covering fols. 23r-43r. Target fol. 4 is omitted, so the object has verified zero overlap with ahb1379.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with ten canvases

entities.label: M. R. James, Lambeth Palace medieval manuscripts catalog (1932)
entities.description: The catalog description supporting MS 131's date, material, extent, hands, first-leaf loss, and broad chronicle placement.

bibliographic.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Lambeth Palace: The Mediaeval Manuscripts (Cambridge: Cambridge University Press, 1932).
bibliographic.title: A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Lambeth Palace: The Mediaeval Manuscripts

entities.description: An aggregate record for Robert of Brunne's two-part Metrical Chronicle. Its five witness entries require part-level qualification before use as Part I ancestry.

rights.basis: The underlying medieval work and all securely identified manuscript witnesses long predate modern copyright terms.

rights.component: Modern XML encoding file as checked into the CME repository
rights.basis: The pinned repository has an Unlicense file whose text refers to software; direct evidence did not establish that its scope includes inherited XML encodings.
notes: This uncertainty is separate from the public-domain status of the medieval text and from U-M's NoC-US item designation.

rights.component: U-M digital item and electronic text
rights.basis: The current U-M item carries the provider's No Copyright - United States designation.
notes: The designation is a provider assertion for the digital item. The XML header's legacy where-applicable copyright warning is preserved as a separate historical statement, not treated as a license.

rights.component: Underlying U-M-derived text represented by the Medictionary XML
rights.basis: The transformed header states that U-M identified the work as public domain and free of known copyright restrictions; this is consistent with the U-M NoC-US item designation.

rights.component: Medictionary-specific wrapper, element transformation, attributes, and presentation markup
rights.basis: No distinct Medictionary license or rights statement for the transformed markup was found in the retrieved XML.
notes: The underlying-text NoC-US/public-domain assertion is not expanded into a license for the transformation layer.

rights.basis: Both physical volume expressions were published in 1887 and are public domain in the United States by publication date.
The volume was published in 1887; the associated Internet Archive metadata also marks possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT.
The volume was published in 1887 and is public domain in the United States by publication date.
The medieval manuscript text predates modern copyright terms.
notes: This status applies only to the historical text, not to photographs, microfilm, photostats, or other modern reproductions.

rights.component: Researcher-created or commissioned photographs and reported modern reproduction layers
rights.basis: Inner Temple limits researcher photography to private study and requires written permission to publish photographed material. Copyright and reuse status for the 1978 World Microfilms copy and Bentley Historical Library MED Records Box 70 photostat were not verified.
notes: Neither collateral reproduction is modeled as Furnivall's source intermediary.

rights.basis: The medieval manuscript text predates modern copyright terms.
notes: This status does not apply to modern Lambeth photographs or ordered reproductions.

rights.component: Ordered digital photographs, PDFs, and other modern reproductions
rights.basis: Lambeth's forms impose private-study conditions on ordered copies and require a separate application for publication or reproduction; they do not provide an open copyright license for the resulting files.
The medieval textual fragment predates modern copyright; no target fol. 4 image license was reviewed because the public IIIF selection omits it.
The medieval text is public domain; provider reproduction terms were not established.
The reported medieval fragment predates modern copyright; provider reproduction terms were not established.
The source volume is public domain and Internet Archive metadata marks the item NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT.
notes: Google Books source/provider terms remain distinct from copyright status.

rights.basis: The scanned source volume was published in 1887 and is public domain in the United States; the item supplies no separate rights or license field.
The U-M digital item carries No Copyright - United States; the image layer remains subject to provider access terms.
Each LUNA record gives Rights: LPL but supplies no open license; Lambeth's reproduction form requires permission for reuse.
notes: Public viewing is not treated as permission to redistribute the cached derivatives.

rights.component: Ten Digital Bodleian photographs, none depicting target fol. 4
rights.basis: The IIIF attribution states Photo © Bodleian Libraries and links CC BY-NC 4.0; the manifest's top-level license field is null, so the attribution is recorded explicitly.
Heidelberg's IIIF manifest applies the Public Domain Mark 1.0 to the digital object.
The record is publicly viewable, but no open license for redistribution of the modern database record was verified.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lambeth's lost opening leaf and local defects in its principal text
editorial_practices.representation: Petyt supplies at exact printed-line scopes, otherwise Lambeth as the principal base from line 199
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall takes lines 1-198 from Petyt, then inserts only the enumerated later Petyt lines, phrases, or lacuna repairs into the Lambeth-based main stream.
editorial_practices.scope: Part I lines 1-13,108 in volume I
notes: Petyt continuation quoted after line 4,918 through line 4,942 belongs to apparatus only and is excluded from the main stream.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Lambeth principal text with one Petyt phrase supply
editorial_practices.representation: Lambeth-based text with per totum psalmum supplied from Petyt at printed line 16,480
editorial_practices.description: The volume II portion of Part I continues Lambeth as base and identifies the one exact Petyt main-text phrase supply.
editorial_practices.scope: Part I lines 13,109-16,688 in volume II

editorial_practices.source_feature: Petyt variants across the target after the initial base passage
editorial_practices.representation: NOTE1 variants and occasional explicitly signaled main-text supplies
editorial_practices.description: Petyt is collated throughout Part I; apparatus-only variants are not treated as main-stream transcription.
editorial_practices.scope: Part I after line 198 across both 1887 volumes

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two printed volumes with front matter, Part I, Part II, apparatus, and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: One uncorrected OCR item with FRONT, BODY, BACK, PB image links, and nested NOTE1 apparatus
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item reproduces the publication-level container. General Corpus therefore imposes an explicit work boundary at line 16,688 rather than treating every encoded BODY line as ahb1379.

editorial_practices.source_feature: U-M-derived DLPSTEXTCLASS XML with specialized TITLEPAGE, NOTE1/NOTE2, Q1, HI1, EPB, LB, NODE, and PB technical attributes
editorial_practices.representation: ETS/EEBO wrapper with flattened title-page DIV1, NOTE PLACE=marg/foot, Q, HI, and reduced source attributes
editorial_practices.description: Medictionary preserves the BODY character stream after whitespace normalization and the core DIV1/LG/L/PB/MILESTONE counts, while transforming document structure and updating the availability paragraph.
notes: Because NOTE1 becomes generic NOTE PLACE=marg, raw direct-versus-apparatus L accounting must inherit the source-layer mapping rather than use the pinned XML's NOTE1 XPath unchanged.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed line labels with a 12,899-to-13,000 jump and a thirteen-row 10,296-10,309 span
editorial_practices.representation: 16,629 direct BODY L, plus 345 apparatus L
editorial_practices.description: The terminal label 16,730 implies 16,630 numbered positions after accounting for the deliberate hundred-label jump, but direct BODY contains 16,629 rows because p. 360 spans thirteen encoded rows across fourteen labels. This is recorded as a numbering anomaly rather than a missing encoded row.

evidence.citation: Medictionary current XML for ahb1379
evidence.locator: complete XML response; HEADER, ETS/EEBO wrapper, FRONT, BODY, BACK, NOTE, Q, HI, PB, and MILESTONE
evidence.summary: Provides a current HTTP-200 structural version of the U-M-derived encoding, an updated public-domain availability paragraph, and the complete target plus Part II transition.

evidence.citation: Structural and textual comparison of the current Medictionary XML with the pinned CME ahb1379 XML
evidence.locator: whole-document element census; BODY string and whitespace-normalized hashes; target and transition boundaries
evidence.summary: Finds identical whitespace-normalized BODY text and identical 278 BODY DIV1, 436 LG, 16,974 L, 880 PB, and 637 MILESTONE counts. Differences include four rather than two FRONT DIV1, 6,132 NOTE replacing 6,128 NOTE1 plus four NOTE2, 110 rather than 100 P after title-page flattening, changed wrappers and emphasis/quotation tags, removed EPB/LB and technical attributes, and a revised availability statement.

evidence.citation: Middle English Compendium / Middle English Dictionary bibliography record BIB2350
evidence.locator: record title, 1338 date, and Part I witness references
evidence.summary: The exact current record used in pre-draft research supports the normalized Part 1 title, 1338 date, Petyt 511.7, Lambeth 131, and Rawlinson D.913 fol. 4 target census, and reports a Petyt photostat at Bentley Historical Library, MED Records Box 70, when read with the cached edition and catalog sources. Current automated retrieval returned a provider 403 challenge, so no local HTML copy or search-result snippet is represented as direct cached evidence.
notes: The access-blocked/cache-negative condition is explicit; directly cached Furnivall, DIMEV, XML, James, and MARCO evidence also support the scoped assertions.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Lambeth Palace: The Mediaeval Manuscripts (1932)
evidence.locator: MS 131 entry
evidence.summary: Describes MS 131 as fifteenth-century paper, 12 x 9 inches, 134 folios, in two hands, with the first leaf lost, and supplies broad chronicle placement.

evidence.citation: Heidelberg University Library digital object for James 1932
evidence.locator: manifest identity, metadata, attribution, license, and canvas sequence
evidence.summary: Confirms the catalog's digital identity, complete image sequence, Heidelberg attribution, DOI, and Public Domain Mark 1.0.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library LUNA search and IIIF collection for Robert of Brunne, MS 131
evidence.locator: five results for MS131f1r, f23v, f26v, f39v, and f42v; Date and Rights fields; per-image IIIF manifest URLs
evidence.summary: Confirms that the public object is exactly five Image records, each dated fifteenth century and marked Rights: LPL, with no Book-level whole-manuscript facsimile.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library digital-photography and permission-to-reproduce forms
evidence.locator: complete forms and conditions
evidence.summary: States private-study restrictions, separate publication permission, fee-bearing image options, preservation limits, and a stated completion time of up to four weeks.

evidence.citation: Bodleian MARCO record for MS. Rawl. D. 913
evidence.locator: physical description, composite contents, provenance, and digital-object link
evidence.summary: Confirms the composite guardbook, its circa-1861 binding context, current shelfmark, and linked Digital Bodleian object. Aggregate dates are not transferred to target fol. 4.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest e587f7ee-c786-4659-8486-c104d5633fe9
evidence.locator: all ten canvas labels and attribution
evidence.summary: Shows ten selected canvases at fols. 23r-43r, proves target fol. 4 is absent, and states Photo © Bodleian Libraries with CC BY-NC 4.0 in the attribution while the top-level license field is null.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian terms of use
evidence.locator: permitted and prohibited uses, commercial restrictions, and licensing contact
evidence.summary: Records provider terms governing Digital Bodleian images and separates them from the manuscript text's copyright status and the manifest attribution license.

evidence.citation: Line-by-line source and structure audit of the pinned XML against both Furnivall volume scans
evidence.locator: printed lines 1-16,730; every Petyt main-text supply marker; NOTE1 continuation; PB and MILESTONE boundaries
evidence.summary: Confirms exact Petyt supply scopes, Lambeth exclusions, the line 16,688 target ending, the forty-two-line transition, direct-versus-apparatus LG/L counts, and both numbering anomalies.

evidence.citation: General Corpus ahb1379 edition, witness, cache, access, and rights synthesis
evidence.locator: entities, relations, access, rights, editorial practices, and open questions
evidence.summary: Documents the evaluated separation of textual witnesses, edition expressions, digital encodings, scan carriers, provider selections, rights assertions, and unresolved questions.

open_questions.question: What exact Lambeth MS 131 folio side and column contains the Part I ending at Furnivall line 16,688?
next_steps: Inspect fol. 71 directly or order the terminal leaves; preserve the current broad through-fol.-71v locator until then.
Do not infer an exact column solely from the next encoded Petyt or Lambeth milestone.

open_questions.question: What is the production date of the target fragment on Rawlinson D.913 fol. 4?
next_steps: Obtain a folio-specific catalog description or inspect fol. 4; do not inherit aggregate dates from the composite MARCO record.
Keep the circa-1861 guardbook assembly date separate from the medieval fragment.

open_questions.question: Does Gloucester Cathedral Library MS 36, fragment 8 overlap Part I, Part II, or both?
next_steps: Obtain a direct catalog entry, transcription, or image before assigning the fragment to the target lineage.

open_questions.question: What production date should be assigned to Petyt 511.7 from a directly reviewable current catalog or codicological study?
next_steps: Consult the Inner Temple manuscript catalog or a direct codicological description before adding a normalized date.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's Unlicense file explicitly cover inherited XML encodings such as ahb1379?
next_steps: Seek a repository-owner clarification or file-level provenance statement before treating the Unlicense as an XML redistribution license.
Continue to separate XML-file rights from the medieval text and U-M item rights statement.

open_questions.question: Can a complete digital facsimile of Lambeth MS 131 be obtained or commissioned?
next_steps: Use the Library's photography-order process for required leaves and seek separate reuse permission before redistribution.
Do not describe the five-image LUNA selection as a whole-manuscript facsimile.

open_questions.question: Can a public or requestable image of Rawlinson D.913 fol. 4 be obtained?
next_steps: Request target fol. 4 from Bodleian imaging services if direct paleographical verification is needed.
Keep the ten-canvas public IIIF object marked target absent.

open_questions.question: Which physical copies supplied the 880-image U-M layer, what item-level OCR or correction history applies beyond the generic header, and does a current complete page-image package or direct delivery route still exist?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for item-level digitization and OCR production records, including the physical-copy provenance of each volume.
Verify a human-usable complete image sequence or package before upgrading access:umich-page-images from unknown.
Keep the U-M image layer distinct from both Internet Archive scan carriers unless copy identity is directly established.

open_questions.question: Can a current public complete or selected facsimile of Inner Temple Petyt 511.7 be verified?
next_steps: Apply to Inner Temple Library for direct manuscript access or current photography options.
Ask about access to the 1978 World Microfilms copy and verify the reported Bentley Historical Library MED Records Box 70 photostat directly.
Establish reuse terms separately; do not treat either collateral reproduction as Furnivall's source intermediary.
